raw_text: I get satisfaction from helping others and if this were taken away from me I would get depressed
clean_text: I get satisfaction from helping others and if this were taken away from me I would get depressed
reasoning: The item directly measures vicarious gratification through giving to others what the self desires, a core feature of altruism. raw_text: People often call me a sulker
clean_text: People often call me a sulker

The item captures the characteristic pattern of expressing aggression through visible withdrawal and non-verbal communication of distress that others recognize ('people call me'), aligning with the codebook's emphasis on oppressive nonverbal broadcasts and current suffering aimed at frustrating others.

raw_text: I'm able to keep a problem out of my mind until I have time to deal with it
clean_text: I'm able to keep a problem out of my mind until I have time to deal with it

raw_text: I work out my anxiety through doing something constructive and creative like painting or woodwork
clean_text: I work out my anxiety through doing something constructive and creative like painting or woodwork

The phrase 'work out...through doing something constructive and creative' matches sublimation's linguistic signals of redirecting impulses into productive forms without suppression.

raw_text: I am able to find good reasons for everything I do
clean_text: I am able to find good reasons for everything I do
reasoning: This item directly captures the essence of rationalization—constructing plausible, socially acceptable reasons to justify behaviors that were actually driven by other motives. The phrase 'find good reasons for everything I do' matches Franklin's description cited in the codebook: enabling one to 'find or make a Reason for everything one has in mind to do,' converting what one already wants into reasonable language.

raw_text: I'm able to laugh at myself pretty easily
clean_text: I'm able to laugh at myself pretty easily

The codebook specifies that humor 'typically makes oneself rather than others the butt of the joke' and involves 'self-deprecating observations about one's pretensions or limitations.'

raw_text: People tend to mistreat me
clean_text: People tend to mistreat me

clean_text: If someone mugged me and stole my money, I'd rather he'd be helped than punished

The wording explicitly targets the idealization process without tapping any secondary mechanism.

raw_text: Didyouconsciouslyorunconsciouslytrytoirritatesomeoneinindirectorannoyingways?
clean_text: Did you consciously or unconsciously try to irritate someone in indirect or annoying ways?
reasoning: This item directly assesses indirect expression of hostility through annoying or irritating behavior aimed at the actual target, which is the defining feature of passive aggression. The emphasis on 'indirect ways' captures the defense's characteristic of communicating aggression while maintaining plausible deniability of hostile intent.

raw_text: Didyoutemporarilyputasideyourpersonalneedstodealwithotherthingsthatneededtobedone?
clean_text: Did you temporarily put aside your personal needs to deal with other things that needed to be done?
reasoning: The item directly operationalizes suppression's core feature: semiconscious, temporary postponement of personal needs/desires while maintaining full awareness of them. The emphasis on 'temporarily' and active decision-making ('put aside') matches suppression's finite deferral rather than elimination, keeping the need in mind while addressing other priorities.

raw_text: Didyoufocusonminororunrelatedmattersthatdistractedyouawayfromaproblemthatmakesyouanxious?
clean_text: Did you focus on minor or unrelated matters that distracted you away from a problem that makes you anxious?
reasoning: The item directly measures redirection of attention and emotional focus from an anxiety-provoking primary problem to less threatening substitute concerns. This matches displacement's core mechanism of shifting preoccupation from its natural object to safer symbolic channels while the affect remains in consciousness.

raw_text: Didyoudiscussanemotionaltopicingeneralorimpersonalway,withoutconsideringorexperiencingyourfeelings?
clean_text: Did you discuss an emotional topic in general or impersonal way, without considering or experiencing your feelings?
reasoning: The item directly measures discussing emotional topics in a detached, impersonal manner without experiencing feelings—the hallmark of intellectualization (talking about feelings like 'weather reports'). Isolation of affect is secondary because the sequestration of affect from cognition underlies this impersonal discussion style.

raw_text: Didyoucomplainabouthowothersdon’tunderstandyouordon’treallycare?
clean_text: Did you complain about how others don't understand you or don't really care?
reasoning: The item directly taps persistent complaining about others' failure to understand or care, which is the core communicative pattern of help-rejecting complaining. This defense maintains aggrieved helplessness through repetitive complaints that simultaneously solicit attention ('others don't understand') while ensuring no genuine relief occurs through the implicit rejection embedded in the complaint itself.

raw_text: Didyouexperiencestrongfeelingstowardsomeone,thinkingthattheotherpersonintendedtomakeyoufeelthatway?
clean_text: Did you experience strong feelings towards someone, thinking that the other person intended to make you feel that way?
reasoning: This item captures the core of projective identification: experiencing strong feelings while maintaining certainty about the other's intentions to induce those feelings, creating the characteristic 'self-fulfilling prophecy' quality where the person both projects internal states and exerts interpersonal pressure on the target to confirm the projection.

raw_text: Didyoufeelconfused,“spacedout,”orunabletotalkaboutadistressingtopic?
clean_text: Did you feel confused, "spaced out," or unable to talk about a distressing topic?
reasoning: The item directly targets alterations in consciousness characteristic of dissociation: feeling "spaced out" maps to retreat into altered states, while being unable to talk about distressing topics indicates compartmentalization of traumatic material through discontinuities in integrated awareness—core definitional features of dissociative defense.

raw_text: Didyouengageinverbalorphysicalfights?
clean_text: Did you engage in verbal or physical fights?
reasoning: The item directly assesses behavioral discharge of aggressive impulses through fighting without reference to conscious emotional processing or deliberation, matching Acting Out's 'direct expression of an unconscious wish or impulse in action' to 'avoid being conscious of the affect.' Secondary Passive Aggression possible if fighting targets the actual source of conflict as current suffering inflicted on another.

raw_text: Didyouhavetroublerememberingsimplethings?
clean_text: Did you have trouble remembering simple things?
reasoning: The item directly assesses motivated forgetting of emotionally charged material, a core feature of repression. The focus on 'simple things' that should be easily recalled but are not maps to repression's dynamic where evidence exists that material has become inaccessible specifically because of its power to upset.

raw_text: Didyouavoidthinkingaboutpersonalproblemsorfeelings?
clean_text: Did you avoid thinking about personal problems or feelings?
reasoning: The item measures conscious, deliberate postponement of attention to unwanted thoughts or affects ('avoid thinking about'), which is the defining feature of suppression—a semiconscious decision to defer rather than eliminate awareness. Unlike repression (unconscious, motivated forgetting), this involves voluntary control over what enters awareness while maintaining the capacity to return to the material later.

raw_text: Didyouperceiveyourselfasverystrong,powerful,untouchable?
clean_text: Did you perceive yourself as very strong, powerful, untouchable?
reasoning: This item directly taps the conviction of possessing unlimited power and being beyond ordinary human constraints ('untouchable'), which are core linguistic signals of Omnipotence. The codebook explicitly includes 'I have special powers that others don't possess' and 'No one's limits apply to me' as characteristic expressions.

raw_text: Didyouhavecontradictoryorconflictualideasaboutatopicthatmakesyouanxious?
clean_text: Did you have contradictory or conflictual ideas about a topic that makes you anxious?
reasoning: The item assesses cognitive awareness of contradictory ideas about anxiety-provoking topics while maintaining intellectual distance from the emotional significance—the person recognizes conflicting thoughts but experiences them as cognitive rather than affective phenomena. This matches isolation of affect's core feature of sequestering cognitive content from its emotional dimension, allowing awareness of conflict without corresponding emotional engagement.

raw_text: Didyoudevalueyourselforothersforyour/theirpersonalcharacteristics?
clean_text: Did you devalue yourself or others for your/their personal characteristics?
reasoning: The item directly assesses the defense mechanism of devaluation by asking about diminishing the worth or qualities of self or others based on personal characteristics. This maps precisely to the codebook definition of devaluation as an operation that protects self-esteem by reducing the perceived value, competence, or power of self or others, typically involving global dismissive characterizations and focus on defects or limitations.

raw_text: I often change my opinion about people; at one time I think highly of them, at another time I think they're worthless
clean_text: I often change my opinion about people; at one time I think highly of them, at another time I think they're worthless
reasoning: The item directly captures the temporal oscillation between all-good ('think highly') and all-bad ('worthless') valuations of the same person without integration, matching the codebook's description of splitting as alternating idealization and devaluation without blending contradictory affects toward the same object.

raw_text: As far as I'm concerned, people are either good or bad
clean_text: As far as I'm concerned, people are either good or bad
reasoning: The item directly operationalizes the core cognitive feature of splitting: segregation of experiences/people into all-good or all-bad categories without integration or ambivalence. The dichotomous 'either good or bad' structure matches the codebook's linguistic signals for categorical assignments and all-or-nothing formulations that characterize this psychotic-level defense.

raw_text: I'm usually able to see the funny side of an otherwise painful predicament
clean_text: I'm usually able to see the funny side of an otherwise painful predicament
reasoning: The item directly measures the capacity to look at painful reality while responding as if it were a game, maintaining full awareness of the distress while finding cathartic pleasure—the core definition of mature humor. The phrase 'funny side of an otherwise painful predicament' captures humor's ability to hold paradox (pain and pleasure) simultaneously without denial or displacement.

raw_text: There's no such thing as 'finding a little good in everyone,' if you're bad, you're all bad
clean_text: There's no such thing as 'finding a little good in everyone,' if you're bad, you're all bad
reasoning: The item directly operationalizes splitting's core mechanism: the inability to integrate positive and negative qualities in the same person, expressed through the all-or-nothing formulation 'if you're bad, you're all bad' and explicit rejection of ambivalence ('no such thing as finding a little good'). This reflects the defense's segregation of experiences into categorical good/bad without acknowledging complexity or allowing contradictory affects to blend.
